description_long: This is a round, earthenware bowl with two looped handles on opposite sides, giving it a symmetrical and functional appearance. The exterior is adorned with geometric patterns in dark brown and black, featuring triangles and diamond motifs arranged in vertical bands. The rim is slightly flared and finished with a lighter, almost beige band, contrasting with the darker body. The interior appears unglazed and is a deep reddish-brown, matching the overall earthy tone of the bowl. The handles are robust and integrated into the body, suggesting the bowl is designed for easy carrying or pouring. The surface finish is matte, with visible texture and hand-painted details, indicating a handcrafted origin.
description: A round, earthenware bowl with two looped handles and geometric patterns in dark brown and black, featuring a matte, textured finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both looped handles and the full geometric pattern banding around the bowl.
description_view_1: Side view with one handle visible and a clear look at the vertical geometric motifs and the lighter rim.
description_view_2: Opposite front view, again showing both handles and the continuous decorative pattern.
description_view_3: Side view with the other handle visible, highlighting the bowl's curvature and pattern continuity.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Geometric patterns with triangles and diamonds in dark brown and black, arranged in vertical bands around the exterior.
